page: Amazon Web Services r8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 192 dedicated physical cores of the custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It utilizes the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.7 GHz, featuring a cache hierarchy of 64 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 36 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core performance and exceptional memory bandwidth. The server achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 291,012.56 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 21,021.0, both placing in the top tier. Memory bandwidth tests show top-tier results with 12,790.27 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 896.26 GB/sec for large-block read operations. Database and memory-bound operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a top-tier Passmark database score of 45,848.51 and Redis SET performance reaching 357.86 million operations per second. However, single-core speeds and text generation for large language models represent relative tradeoffs, with LLM text generation for a 70B model scoring in the weak tier at 2.73 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8g.metal-48xl offers high resource density and massive memory capacity on an ARM64 architecture, which typically delivers favorable performance-per-watt and resource-density tradeoffs compared to traditional x86 architectures. Given the lack of bundled local storage and GPUs, this bare-metal instance is best suited for memory-intensive, highly parallelized workloads. Appropriate applications include large-scale in-memory databases like Redis, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and memory-bound enterprise applications that benefit from direct hardware access without hypervisor overhead.
description: Amazon Web Services r8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated physical ARM64 cores powered by the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory, it provides 8.0 GB of RAM per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, as demonstrated by a stress-ng multi-core score of 291,012.56 and a Redis SET throughput of 357.86 million operations per second. While single-core speeds and LLM text generation represent performance tradeoffs, its massive memory capacity and high parallel processing capability offer excellent qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, and intensive software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized ARM64 server with 192 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, built for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal ARM64 server with 192 cores and 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density, bare-metal ARM64 platform delivering massive DDR5 memory bandwidth and 192 dedicated physical cores for parallel, memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 192 dedicated physical AWS Neoverse-V2 ARM64 cores at 2.7 GHz
Equipped with 1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core processing performance
Features 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis, and software compilation
